action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 21 failed with a state_dict shape mismatch because model.py was corrupted and no longer matches the checkpoint architecture (the checkpoint expects mmsegmentation UPerHead with 512-dim lateral/FPN 3×3 convs, but the current model defines 1×1 convs directly on backbone channels). Best official mIoU remains 0.5132 from iteration 16. With ~67 minutes and 9 iterations left, retraining is impossible; the only lever is restoring the exact architecture and running the proven multi-scale TTA inference pipeline. Analysis: The checkpoint was originally trained with timm ConvNeXt-Large + mmseg UPerHead + FCNHead auxiliary head. I must reconstruct model.py using these exact classes so the checkpoint loads cleanly. To finish safely within the remaining hour, predict.py will use the 7 proven scales but cap max_size at 2400 and batch original+flip together in each forward pass, which roughly halves GPU work versus the unbatched run that produced 0.5132. Decision: Write a correct model.py with the exact mmseg head configuration, write a complete predict.py with the safe TTA pipeline, run a 10-second load+forward sanity check, and immediately submit eval to recover/replace the best score.
action.command:
python -c "
import torch
from model import SegmentationModel
m = SegmentationModel(num_classes=150)
ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu')
sd = ckpt.get('model', ckpt)
m.load_state_dict({k[7:] if k.startswith('module.') else k: v for k,v in sd.items()}, strict=False)
m.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    y = m(torch.randn(1,3,512,512))
    print('LOAD_OK', y.shape)
" && echo "Sanity passed"
